name: Agriculture (Zimbabwe)
definition: Businesses producing crops, livestock, poultry and fish for sale, together with directly related on-farm services; primary production rather than downstream agro-processing.

subSectors.name: Field crops
subSectors.description: Maize, tobacco, wheat, soya beans, cotton and similar arable crops.

subSectors.name: Horticulture
subSectors.description: Fruit, vegetables and flowers, including export horticulture.

subSectors.name: Livestock
subSectors.description: Beef, dairy, pig and small-livestock production.

subSectors.name: Poultry
subSectors.description: Broiler and layer production and day-old-chick supply.

subSectors.name: Agri-services
subSectors.description: Tillage, spraying, harvesting and agronomy services to farms.

typicalBusinessSizes.description: Smallholder and communal producers selling surplus.
Small commercial farms and intensive poultry/horticulture units.
Commercial farms with permanent labour and mechanisation.
Estates, corporate farms and integrated producers.

officialSizeThresholds.note: Official farm-size or MSME classification bands (including the land-reform A1/A2 model definitions) were not verified from an official source in this revision; source before product use.

notes: Descriptive segmentation for product design; not an official statistical classification. Agro-processing is treated as manufacturing and excluded from this seed.
